questions.q: Which constitutional office did T.N. Seshan hold from 1990 to 1996?
questions.explanation: T.N. Seshan served as Chief Election Commissioner from 1990 to 1996.

questions.q: T.N. Seshan is especially associated with the enforcement of which set of election guidelines?
questions.explanation: He is known for vigorously enforcing the model code of conduct during elections.

questions.q: The institution whose authority Seshan's tenure strengthened was the:
questions.explanation: His tenure strengthened the autonomy and authority of the Election Commission.

questions.q: In which year did T.N. Seshan become Chief Election Commissioner?
questions.explanation: Seshan became Chief Election Commissioner in 1990.

questions.q: In which year did T.N. Seshan's tenure as Chief Election Commissioner end?
questions.explanation: His tenure as Chief Election Commissioner ended in 1996.

questions.q: The 'T.N.' in T.N. Seshan's name reflects roots in which southern Indian state?
questions.explanation: Tirunellai Narayana Iyer Seshan hailed from Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: To which service did T.N. Seshan belong before heading the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Seshan was a member of the Indian Administrative Service before becoming Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.q: The office Seshan held is headed by the official titled the:
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is headed by the Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.q: The model code of conduct Seshan enforced applies primarily during which period?
options: The election campaign and polling period
questions.explanation: The model code of conduct applies during the election period to ensure free and fair polls.

questions.q: Seshan's reputation rests on enhancing which quality of the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: His tenure is associated with strengthening the autonomy and authority of the Election Commission.

questions.q: Under which article of the Constitution does the Election Commission that Seshan headed derive its powers of superintendence over elections?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence, direction and control of elections in the Election Commission.

questions.q: Which senior post in the Government of India did Seshan hold shortly before becoming Chief Election Commissioner?
questions.explanation: Seshan served as Cabinet Secretary before his appointment as Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.q: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner, the post Seshan held?
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner is appointed by the President of India.

questions.q: During Seshan's tenure, in 1993, the Election Commission was expanded into which structure?
options: A multi-member body with Election Commissioners
questions.explanation: In 1993 the Commission became a multi-member body with additional Election Commissioners alongside the CEC.

questions.q: Seshan promoted the use of which document to help verify voter identity at polling stations?
questions.explanation: Seshan pushed for the introduction of the elector's photo identity card (EPIC) to verify voter identity.

questions.q: The Election Commission Seshan headed is best described as which type of body?
options: An advisory committee of the Cabinet
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is an autonomous constitutional body.

questions.q: In 1996, Seshan was a recipient of which international award for public service?
questions.explanation: Seshan received the Ramon Magsaysay Award for Government Service in 1996.

questions.q: The Election Commission Seshan led administers elections to all of the following EXCEPT:
options: The offices of President and Vice-President
questions.explanation: The Election Commission conducts elections to Parliament, state legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President, but not cooperative banks.

questions.q: Seshan's enforcement work aimed mainly to ensure elections that were:
questions.explanation: His enforcement of the code of conduct aimed to ensure free and fair elections.

questions.q: The general election of 1996, conducted during Seshan's tenure, returned which type of legislature?
questions.explanation: The 1996 general election, held during his tenure, constituted the eleventh Lok Sabha.

questions.q: After leaving the Election Commission, Seshan contested an election for which high office in 1997?
questions.explanation: In 1997 Seshan contested the presidential election but was not elected.

questions.q: The removal procedure protecting the Chief Election Commissioner, the office Seshan held, is similar to that of:
options: A judge of the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed only by a procedure similar to that for a Supreme Court judge.

questions.q: Which amendment created the post of additional Election Commissioners, leading to the multi-member Commission during Seshan's term?
options: A 1993 legislative change to the Election Commission's composition
questions.explanation: A 1993 change to the law on the Commission's composition added Election Commissioners, making it a multi-member body.

questions.q: Seshan was the Chief Election Commissioner numbered in what position in the line of holders of that office?
questions.explanation: Seshan was the tenth Chief Election Commissioner of India.

questions.q: The dispute over the powers of the CEC versus the new Election Commissioners during Seshan's tenure was settled by which body?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court adjudicated the dispute over the relative powers of the CEC and the Election Commissioners.

questions.q: The model code of conduct that Seshan enforced is best described as what kind of instrument?
options: A set of guidelines agreed upon and enforced by the Commission
A criminal statute passed by Parliament
questions.explanation: The model code of conduct is a set of guidelines evolved and enforced by the Election Commission rather than a statute.

questions.q: Seshan's term as Chief Election Commissioner ended in 1996 chiefly because of which factor?
questions.explanation: His tenure ended in 1996 on completion of the term fixed for the office.

questions.q: Which earlier central ministry portfolio did Seshan administer as a senior civil servant before heading the Commission?
questions.explanation: Seshan held senior posts including Secretary of the Ministry of Environment and Forests during his administrative career.

questions.q: The strengthening of the Election Commission under Seshan is often cited as an example of which constitutional value being asserted?
options: The independence of an autonomous institution
The supremacy of the executive over courts
The merger of legislature and judiciary
questions.explanation: His tenure is cited as an assertion of the independence of an autonomous constitutional institution.

questions.q: During Seshan's tenure, the introduction of the elector's photo identity card was aimed primarily at preventing what?
questions.explanation: The photo identity card was introduced to curb impersonation and bogus voting at the polls.
